title: senecio anconquijae
intro: Senecio anconquijae, also known as Mountain Ragwort, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and native to the high-altitude grasslands of the Andes. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to expel tapeworms and other parasites from the digestive system. The health benefits of Senecio anconquijae include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various ailments such as fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Senecio anconquijae typically involve drying the leaves and roots, then using them in tea or tincture form to harness its medicinal properties.
